parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 1–13.

passage.text:
Research using twins
To biomedical researchers all over the world, twins offer a precious opportunity to untangle the influence of genes and the environment – of nature and nurture. Because identical twins come from a single fertilized egg that splits into two, they share virtually the same genetic code. Any differences between them -one twin having younger looking skin, for example – must be due to environmental factors such as less time spent in the sun.
Alternatively, by comparing the experiences of identical twins with those of fraternal twins, who come from separate eggs and share on average half their DNA, researchers can quantify the extent to which our genes affect our lives. If identical twins are more similar to each other with respect to an ailment than fraternal twins are, then vulnerability to the disease must be rooted at least in part in heredity.
These two lines of research – studying the differences between identical twins to pinpoint the influence of environment, and comparing identical twins with fraternal ones to measure the role of inheritance – have been crucial to understanding the interplay of nature and nurture in determining our personalities, behavior, and vulnerability to disease.
The idea of using twins to measure the influence of heredity dates back to 1875, when the English scientist Francis Galton first suggested the approach (and coined the phrase ‘nature and nurture’). But twin studies took a surprising twist in the 1980s, with the arrival of studies into identical twins who had been separated at birth and reunited as adults. Over two decades 137 sets of twins eventually visited Thomas Bouchard’s lab in what became known as the Minnesota Study of Twins Reared Apart. Numerous tests were carried out on the twins, and they were each asked more than 15,000 questions.
Bouchard and his colleagues used this mountain of data to identify how far twins were affected by their genetic makeup. The key to their approach was a statistical concept called heritability. In broad terms, the heritability of a trait measures the extent to which differences among members of a population can be explained by differences in their genetics. And wherever Bouchard and other scientists looked, it seemed, they found the invisible hand of genetic influence helping to shape our lives.
Lately, however, twin studies have helped lead scientists to a radical new conclusion: that nature and nurture are not the only elemental forces at work. According to a recent field called epigenetics, there is a third factor also in play, one that in some cases serves as a bridge between the environment and our genes, and in others operates on its own to shape who we are.
Epigenetic processes are chemical reactions tied to neither nature nor nurture but representing what researchers have called a ‘third component’. These reactions influence how our genetic code is expressed: how each gene is strengthened or weakened, even turned on or off, to build our bones, brains and all the other parts of our bodies.
If you think of our DNA as an immense piano keyboard and our genes as the keys – each key symbolizing a segment of DNA responsible for a particular note, or trait, and all the keys combining to make us who we are – then epigenetic processes determine when and how each key can be struck, changing the tune being played.
One way the study of epigenetics is revolutionizing our understanding of biology is by revealing a mechanism by which the environment directly impacts on genes. Studies of animals, for example, have shown that when a rat experiences stress during pregnancy, it can cause epigenetic changes in a fetus that lead to behavioral problems as the rodent grows up. Other epigenetic processes appear to occur randomly, while others are normal, such as those that guide embryonic cells as they become heart, brain, or liver cells, for example.
Geneticist Danielle Reed has worked with many twins over the years and thought deeply about what twin studies have taught us. ‘It’s very clear when you look at twins that much of what they share is hardwired,’ she says. ‘Many things about them are absolutely the same and unalterable. But it’s also clear, when you get to know them, that other things about them are different. Epigenetics is the origin of a lot of those differences, in my view.’
Reed credits Thomas Bouchard’s work for today’s surge in twin studies. ‘He was the trailblazer,’ she says. ‘We forget that 50 years ago things like heart disease were thought to be caused entirely by lifestyle. Schizophrenia was thought to be due to poor mothering. Twin studies have allowed us to be more reflective about what people are actually born with and what’s caused by experience.’
Having said that, Reed adds, the latest work in epigenetics promises to take our understanding even further. ‘What I like to say is that nature writes some things in pencil and some things in pen,’ she says. ‘Things written in pen you can’t change. That’s DNA. But things written in pencil you can. That’s epigenetics. Now that we’re actually able to look at the DNA and see where the pencil writings are, it’s sort of a whole new world.’

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 1–4 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this

questions.prompt: There may be genetic causes for the differences in how young the skin of identical twins looks.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “genetic causes” (nguyên nhân di truyền), “differences”, “how young the skin... looks”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1: “Any differences between them - one twin having younger-looking skin, for example - must be due to environmental factors such as less time spent in the sun.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn khẳng định bất kỳ sự khác biệt nào giữa cặp song sinh cùng trứng — ví dụ như một người có làn da trông trẻ trung hơn — bắt buộc phải là do các yếu tố môi trường (must be due to environmental factors) như việc ít tiếp xúc với ánh nắng mặt trời, chứ hoàn toàn không phải do mã di truyền (vì họ có chung một mã di truyền giống hệt nhau). Phát biểu mang thông tin trái ngược.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questions.prompt: Twins are at greater risk of developing certain illnesses than non-twins.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “greater risk” (nguy cơ cao hơn), “developing certain illnesses”, “than non-twins” (so với người không sinh đôi)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2 & Đoạn 3: Các đoạn này có nhắc đến cụm “vulnerability to the disease” và “ailment”.
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ đề cập đến việc so sánh mức độ mắc bệnh giữa cặp song sinh cùng trứng và khác trứng nhằm xác định xem căn bệnh đó có phải do di truyền hay không. Tuyệt đối không có bất kỳ thông tin nào so sánh nguy cơ mắc bệnh giữa người sinh đôi với người không sinh đôi (non-twins).
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Bouchard advertised in newspapers for twins who had been separated at birth.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Bouchard”, “advertised in newspapers” (quảng cáo trên báo), “separated at birth”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4 & Đoạn 5: Nói về nghiên cứu của Bouchard (Minnesota Study of Twins Reared Apart).
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài viết có nêu thông tin trong suốt hai thập kỷ đã có 137 cặp song sinh bị tách rời từ nhỏ đến tham gia phòng thí nghiệm của Thomas Bouchard. Tuy nhiên, việc ông thu hút hay tìm kiếm họ bằng cách nào (có phải qua quảng cáo trên báo — advertised in newspapers hay không) thì bài đọc hoàn toàn không nhắc tới.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Epigenetic processes are different from both genetic and environmental processes.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Epigenetic processes”, “different from both”, “genetic and environmental processes”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 6 & Đoạn 7: “According to a recent field called epigenetics, there is a third factor also in play... Epigenetic processes are chemical reactions tied to neither nature nor nurture but representing what researchers have called a 'third component'.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Quá trình biểu sinh (epigenetics) được khẳng định là không gắn liền với tự nhiên (nature — yếu tố di truyền/gen) cũng không thuộc về sự nuôi dưỡng (nurture — yếu tố môi trường), mà đóng vai trò độc lập như một "thành phần thứ ba" (third component). Vì vậy, nó hoàn toàn khác biệt với hai quá trình trên.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questionGroups.instruction:
Match each statement with the correct option, A–C.
Write the correct letter in boxes 5–9 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: invented a term used to distinguish two factors affecting human characteristics
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “invented a term” (phát minh/đặt ra thuật ngữ), “distinguish two factors” (phân biệt hai yếu tố)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “...when the English scientist Francis Galton first suggested the approach (and coined the phrase 'nature and nurture').”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Động từ “coined the phrase” đồng nghĩa hoàn toàn với cụm “invented a term”. Người đầu tiên nghĩ ra và sử dụng cụm từ "nature and nurture" (tự nhiên và nuôi dưỡng — hai yếu tố chính tác động đến con người) chính là Francis Galton.
=> Đáp án: A (Francis Galton)

questions.prompt: expressed the view that the study of epigenetics will increase our knowledge
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “study of epigenetics”, “increase our knowledge” (mở rộng/nâng cao kiến thức nhận thức)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 10 & Đoạn 12: “...the latest work in epigenetics promises to take our understanding even further... it's sort of a whole new world.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Ở cuối bài, nhà di truyền học Danielle Reed đã đưa ra nhận định rằng những công trình nghiên cứu mới nhất về biểu sinh hứa hẹn sẽ đưa mức độ hiểu biết của chúng ta đi xa hơn nữa (take our understanding even further = increase our knowledge).
=> Đáp án: C (Danielle Reed)

questions.prompt: developed a mathematical method of measuring genetic influences
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “developed a mathematical method” (phát triển phương pháp toán học), “measuring genetic influences” (đo lường ảnh hưởng di truyền).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 5 của bài đọc:

“Bouchard and his colleagues used this mountain of data to identify how far twins were affected by their genetic makeup. The key to their approach was a statistical concept called heritability. In broad terms, the heritability of a trait measures the extent to which differences among members of a population can be explained by differences in their genetics.”  

Bước 3: Phân tích: * Cụm từ “statistical concept” (khái niệm thống kê — một nhánh của toán học ứng dụng) chính là cách paraphrase của “mathematical method”.

Cụm “measures the extent to which... differences in their genetics” tương ứng hoàn toàn với “measuring genetic influences”.

Phương pháp/cách tiếp cận này là chìa khóa (The key to their approach) trong nghiên cứu của Thomas Bouchard và các đồng nghiệp khi họ xử lý khối lượng dữ liệu khổng lồ từ các cặp song sinh.

Còn Francis Galton ở đoạn 4 chỉ là người đầu tiên đề xuất ý tưởng dùng cặp song sinh (suggested the approach) và đưa ra thuật ngữ "nature and nurture" chứ chưa cụ thể hóa bằng công cụ toán học thống kê này.

=> Đáp án: B (Thomas Bouchard)

questions.prompt: pioneered research into genetics using twins
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “pioneered” (người đi tiên phong, người đặt viên gạch đầu tiên), “research into genetics using twins” (nghiên cứu di truyền học bằng cách dùng cặp song sinh).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4 của bài đọc:

“The idea of using twins to measure the influence of heredity dates back to 1875, when the English scientist Francis Galton first suggested the approach and coined the phrase ‘nature and nurture’.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: * Cụm “first suggested the approach” (lần đầu tiên đề xuất hướng tiếp cận này) vào tận năm 1875 chính là định nghĩa hoàn hảo nhất cho động từ “pioneered” (đi tiên phong trong lịch sử). Francis Galton là người đầu tiên nghĩ ra việc dùng cặp song sinh để nghiên cứu di truyền.

Tại sao không chọn Thomas Bouchard (mặc dù có từ trailblazer)? Ở đoạn cuối bài, Danielle Reed nói Bouchard là một “trailblazer” cho “today's surge in twin studies” (sự bùng nổ/sự gia tăng mạnh mẽ của các nghiên cứu song sinh ngày nay). Nghĩa là Bouchard có công lớn trong việc vực dậy và làm cho các nghiên cứu này bùng nổ ở thời hiện đại, chứ ông ấy không phải là người đầu tiên khai phá ra phương pháp này từ quá khứ.
=> Đáp án : A (Francis Galton)

questions.prompt: carried out research into twins who had lived apart
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “carried out research”, “twins who had lived apart” (những cặp sinh đôi sống tách biệt nhau)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 4: “Over two decades 137 sets of twins eventually visited Thomas Bouchard's lab in what became known as the Minnesota Study of Twins Reared Apart.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Cụm từ “lived apart” tương ứng với từ “Reared Apart” (được nuôi dưỡng ở những nơi tách biệt nhau). Người trực tiếp thực hiện chuỗi khảo sát, thí nghiệm quy mô lớn này tại phòng lab của mình chính là Thomas Bouchard.
=> Đáp án: B (Thomas Bouchard)

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–F, below.

questions.prompt: Epigenetic processes act as a sort of ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “epigenetic processes”, “influence the activity of our genes”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7:
“Epigenetic processes are chemical reactions tied to neither nature nor nurture... These reactions influence how our genetic code is expressed...”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ ra bản chất của các quá trình biểu sinh (epigenetic processes) thực chất là các phản ứng hóa học (chemical reactions). Chính những phản ứng này chi phối và gây ảnh hưởng lên cách thức mã di truyền hay các gen hoạt động. Do đó, tác nhân chính ở đây là các chất hóa học (chemicals).
=> Đáp án: D (chemicals)

questions.prompt: for example in creating our internal 11 _______ .
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “for example”, “creating”, “internal” (bên trong)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 7:
“...how each gene is strengthened or weakened, even turned on or off, to build our bones, brains and all the other parts of our bodies.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Đoạn văn lấy ví dụ các phản ứng này tác động để cấu thành nên xương (bones), não bộ (brains) và tất cả những bộ phận khác của cơ thể chúng ta. Những thành phần cấu tạo sâu bên trong cơ thể này được gọi chung bằng thuật ngữ các cơ quan nội tạng (organs).
=> Đáp án: B (organs)

questions.prompt: The study of epigenetic processes is uncovering a way in which our genes can be affected by our 12 _______ .
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: *“uncovering a way”, “genes can be affected by our” (gen bị tác động bởi...)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 9: “One way the study of epigenetics is revolutionizing our understanding of biology is by revealing a mechanism by which the environment directly impacts on genes.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Nghiên cứu biểu sinh đã làm sáng tỏ một cơ chế mới, cho thấy các yếu tố tới từ môi trường bên ngoài có khả năng tác động và ảnh hưởng trực tiếp lên gen (the environment directly impacts on genes). Do đó, từ cần chọn là environment (đáp án E).
=> Đáp án: E (environment)

questions.prompt: One example is that if a pregnant rat suffers stress, the new-born rat may later show problems in its 13 _______ .
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “pregnant rat suffers stress”, “new-born rat”, “show problems in its”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 9: “...when a rat experiences stress during pregnancy, it can cause epigenetic changes in a fetus that lead to behavioral problems as the rodent grows up.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi chuột mẹ bị căng thẳng lúc mang thai sẽ để lại những biến đổi sinh học ở thai nhi, dẫn tới việc khi chuột con lớn lên, chúng gặp phải các vấn đề liên quan tới mặt hành vi (behavioral problems). Từ tương ứng trong bảng từ chọn là behaviour (đáp án F).
=> Đáp án: 13 - F (behaviour)

questionGroups.note:
Epigenetic processes

In epigenetic processes, 10________ influence the activity of our genes, for example in creating our internal 11________. The study of epigenetic processes is uncovering a way in which our genes can be affected by our 12________. One example is that if a pregnant rat suffers stress, the new-born rat may later show problems in its 13________.

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 14–26.

passage.text:
An Introduction to Film Sound
Though we might think of film as an essentially visual experience, we really cannot afford to underestimate the importance of film sound. A meaningful sound track is often as complicated as the image on the screen, and is ultimately just as much the responsibility of the director. The entire sound track consists of three essential ingredients: the human voice, sound effects and music. These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects. Topics which essentially refer to the three previously mentioned tracks are discussed below. They include dialogue, synchronous and asynchronous sound effects, and music.
Let us start with dialogue. As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well. Often with film characterization the audience perceives little or no difference between the character and the actor. Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge. Perhaps this is because the very texture of a performer’s voice supplies an element of character.
When voice textures fit the performer’s physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges. The viewer sees not an actor working at his craft, but another human being struggling with life. It is interesting to note that how dialogue is used and the very amount of dialogue used varies widely among films. For example, in the highly successful science-fiction film 2001, little dialogue was evident, and most of it was banal and of little intrinsic interest. In this way the film-maker was able to portray what Thomas Sobochack and Vivian Sobochack call, in An Introduction to Film, the ‘inadequacy of human responses when compared with the magnificent technology created by man and the visual beauties of the universe’.
The comedy Bringing Up Baby, on the other hand, presents practically non-stop dialogue delivered at breakneck speed. This use of dialogue underscores not only the dizzy quality of the character played by Katherine Hepburn, but also the absurdity of the film itself and thus its humor. The audience is bounced from gag to gag and conversation to conversation; there is no time for audience reflection. The audience is caught up in a whirlwind of activity in simply managing to follow the plot. This film presents pure escapism – largely due to its frenetic dialogue.
Synchronous sound effects are those sounds which are synchronized or matched with what is viewed. For example, if the film portrays a character playing the piano, the sounds of the piano are projected. Synchronous sounds contribute to the realism of film and also help to create a particular atmosphere. For example, the ‘click’ of a door being opened may simply serve to convince the audience that the image portrayed is real, and the audience may only subconsciously note the expected sound. However, if the ‘click’ of an opening door is part of an ominous action such as a burglary, the sound mixer may call attention to the ‘click’ with an increase in volume; this helps to engage the audience in a moment of suspense.
Asynchronous sound effects, on the other hand, are not matched with a visible source of the sound on screen. Such sounds are included so as to provide an appropriate emotional nuance, and they may also add to the realism of the film. For example, a film-maker might opt to include the background sound of an ambulance’s siren while the foreground sound and image portrays an arguing couple. The asynchronous ambulance siren underscores the psychic injury incurred in the argument; at the same time the noise of the siren adds to the realism of the film by acknowledging the film’s city setting.
We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence. We are aware that it is used to add emotion and rhythm. Usually not meant to be noticeable, it often provides a tone or an emotional attitude toward the story and /or the characters depicted. In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood. For example, dissonant music may be used in film to indicate an approaching (but not yet visible) menace or disaster.
Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes. For example, a particular musical theme associated with an individual character or situation may be repeated at various points in a film in order to remind the audience of salient motifs or ideas.
Film sound comprises conventions and innovations. We have come to expect an acceleration of music during car chases and creaky doors in horror films. Yet, it is important to note as well that sound is often brilliantly conceived. The effects of sound are often largely subtle and often are noted by only our subconscious minds. We need to foster an awareness of film sound as well as film space so as to truly appreciate an art form that sprang to life during the twentieth century – the modern film.

questions.prompt: In the first paragraph, the writer makes a point that
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “first paragraph” (đoạn 1), “makes a point” (đưa ra quan điểm)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 1:
“Though we might think of film as an essentially visual experience, we really cannot afford to underestimate the importance of film sound.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Câu văn nhấn mạnh: Mặc dù chúng ta thường coi phim ảnh cốt lõi là một trải nghiệm về mặt thị giác (visual experience), chúng ta thực sự không nên/không thể đánh giá thấp tầm quan trọng của âm thanh trong phim (cannot afford to underestimate the importance of film sound). Cụm “underestimate” (đánh giá thấp) đồng nghĩa hoàn toàn với “overlook” (bỏ qua/ngó lơ). Do đó, việc đánh giá thấp âm thanh là một điều sai lầm.
=> Đáp án: B (it would be wrong to overlook the contribution of sound to the artistry of films.)

options.label: A. The director should plan the soundtrack at an early stage in filming.
B. It would be wrong to overlook the contribution of sound to the artistry of films.
C. The music industry can have a beneficial influence on sound in film.
D. It is important for those working on the sound in a film to have sole responsibility for it.

questions.prompt: One reason that the writer refers to Humphrey Bogart is to exemplify
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Humphrey Bogart”, “exemplify” (minh họa cho điều gì)
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn 2:
“Often with film characterization the audience perceives little or no difference between the character and the actor. Thus, for example, the actor Humphrey Bogart is the character Sam Spade; film personality and life personality seem to merge.”  
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả lấy ví dụ về diễn viên Humphrey Bogart để minh họa cho hiện tượng: khán giả hầu như nhận thấy rất ít hoặc không có sự khác biệt giữa nhân vật và diễn viên (perceives little or no difference between the character and the actor), tính cách trên phim và tính cách ngoài đời thực của họ như hòa vào làm một (personality seem to merge). Điều này tương đồng với ý: diễn viên và nhân vật có tính cách tương đồng nhau (similar personalities).
=> Đáp án: A (the importance of the actor and the character appearing to have similar personalities.)

options.label: the importance of the actor and the character appearing to have similar personalities.
the audience’s wish that actors are visually appropriate for their roles.
the value of the actor having had similar feelings to the character.
the audience’s preference for dialogue to be as authentic as possible.

questions.prompt: In the third paragraph, the writer suggests that
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “third paragraph”, “suggests”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C, câu thứ 4:
“For example, in the highly successful science-fiction film 2001, little dialogue was evident, and most of it was banal and of little intrinsic interest.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả chỉ ra bộ phim 2001 sử dụng rất ít lời thoại, và phần lớn lời thoại đó đều mang tính chất sáo rỗng (banal), không có giá trị nổi bật. Tuy nhiên, sự sáo rỗng này không phải ngẫu nhiên mà được thiết kế có chủ ý để phục vụ đúng nội dung, ý đồ nghệ thuật cốt truyện của phim.
=> Đáp án: B

options.label: audiences are likely to be critical of film dialogue that does not reflect their own experience.
film dialogue that appears to be dull may have a specific purpose.
filmmakers vary considerably in the skill with which they handle dialogue.
the most successful films are those with dialogue of a high Quality.

questions.prompt: What does the writer say about 'foreshadowing' in film sound?
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “foreshadowing”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn D, câu cuối cùng:
“This film presents pure escapism – largely due to its frenetic dialogue.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả giải thích bộ phim Bringing Up Baby mang lại cảm giác thoát ly thực tế (escapism) đỉnh cao cho khán giả chủ yếu là nhờ vào các đoạn hội thoại liên tục, dồn dập cuồng nhiệt với tốc độ chóng mặt (frenetic dialogue). Lối hội thoại này tập trung hướng người xem vào nhịp điệu để đạt mục đích giải trí.
=> Đáp án: D

options.label: The plot suffers from the filmmaker’s wish to focus on humorous dialogue.
The dialogue helps to make it one of the best comedy films ever produced.
There is a mismatch between the speed of the dialogue and the speed of actions.
The nature of the dialogue emphasises key elements of the film.

questions.prompt: The writer refers to the ‘click’ of a door to make the point that realistic sounds
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “click of a door”, “realistic sounds” (âm thanh thực tế/chân thực).
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Nhìn ngay vào đoạn văn tiếng Anh ở góc trên bên phải hình bạn chụp, câu đầu tiên (ngay trước chữ However màu xanh):

“For example, the ‘click’ of a door being opened may simply serve to convince the audience that the image portrayed is real, and the audience may only subconsciously note the expected sound.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Tác giả nói tiếng "cạch" cửa khi mở chỉ đơn thuần phục vụ mục đích thuyết phục khán giả rằng hình ảnh trên phim là thật (biến bộ phim trở nên thực tế), và khán giả thường chỉ nhận biết âm thanh quen thuộc này một cách vô thức/trong tiềm thức (subconsciously).
=> Đáp án: C

options.label: are often used to give the audience a false impression of events in the film.
may be interpreted in different ways by different members of the audience.
may be modified in order to manipulate the audience’s response to the film.
tend to be more significant in films presenting realistic situations.

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the information given in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 19–23 on your answer sheet, write
TRUE  if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE  if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN  if there is no information on this

questions.prompt: Audiences are likely to be surprised if a film lacks background music.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Audiences”, “surprised”, “lacks background music”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G, câu đầu tiên:
“We are probably all familiar with background music in films, which has become so ubiquitous as to be noticeable in its absence.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Nhạc nền phổ biến tới mức chỉ cần nó vắng mặt (absence = lacks) là khán giả sẽ lập tức nhận ra ngay (noticeable). Khi một yếu tố quen thuộc đột ngột biến mất khiến người ta giật mình chú ý, điều đó đồng nghĩa với việc khán giả sẽ cảm thấy ngạc nhiên/bất ngờ. Thông tin hoàn toàn trùng khớp.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Background music may anticipate a development in a film.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “Background music”, “anticipate”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G, câu thứ 4 và 5:
“In addition, background music often foreshadows a change in mood. For example, dissonant music may be used in film to indicate an approaching (but not yet visible) menace or disaster.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Nhạc nền thường báo trước (foreshadows = anticipate) diễn biến tiếp theo. Ví dụ, âm thanh chói tai, nghịch âm báo hiệu một mối đe dọa hoặc thảm họa đang đến gần dù hình ảnh chưa xuất hiện trên màn hình, giúp khán giả đoán trước được tình tiết sắp tới.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Background music has more effect on some people than on others.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “manufactured by a film studio”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G và Đoạn H (các đoạn thảo luận về nhạc nền).
Bước 3: Phân tích: Trong toàn bộ mạch bài đọc, tác giả chỉ phân tích sâu về công năng, vai trò tác động tâm lý và tính nghệ thuật của nhạc nền đối với người xem. Không có bất kỳ câu chữ nào đề cập hay cung cấp thông tin về việc nguồn nhạc nền này do ai sản xuất hay được chế tạo bởi hãng phim (film studio) nào.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: Background music may help the audience to make certain connections within the film
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “more than one function”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn G, câu thứ 2 từ dưới lên:
“Background music may aid viewer understanding by linking scenes.”
Bước 3: Phân tích: Ngoài chức năng dự báo tâm trạng ở câu trên, tác giả liệt kê thêm chức năng khác của nhạc nền là hỗ trợ khán giả hiểu nội dung phim bằng cách kết nối, tạo sự liên kết mạch lạc giữa các cảnh quay quay (linking scenes). Do đó, nhạc nền rõ ràng có nhiều hơn một chức năng.
=> Đáp án: TRUE

questions.prompt: Audiences tend to be aware of how the background music is affecting them
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “aware” (nhận thức được), “background music is affecting them” (nhạc nền đang tác động đến họ).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn H, câu thứ 4 (vùng bôi xanh lá ở cột phải):

“The effects of sound are often largely subtle and often are noted by only our subconscious minds.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: * Bài đọc khẳng định hiệu ứng của âm thanh (bao gồm cả nhạc nền) thường rất tinh tế (subtle) và chỉ được ghi nhận bởi tiềm thức của chúng ta (noted by only our subconscious minds).

Tiềm thức (subconscious) thì hoàn toàn trái ngược với việc chủ động nhận thức rõ ràng (be aware). Con người không hề nhận biết hay có ý thức rõ về việc nhạc nền đang điều khiển cảm xúc của mình.
=> Đáp án: FALSE

questionGroups.instruction:
Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–E, below.
Write the correct letter in boxes 24–26 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: The audience’s response to different parts of a film can be controlled ______
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “audience’s response”, “can be controlled”
Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn A, câu thứ 3:
“The entire sound track consists of three essential ingredients: the human voice, sound effects and music. These three tracks must be mixed and balanced so as to produce the necessary emphases which in turn create desired effects.”  
Bước 3: Phân tích: Khi ba yếu tố âm thanh được phối trộn và cân bằng một cách hợp lý, chúng sẽ tạo ra các điểm nhấn cần thiết để đạt được hiệu ứng tâm lý như mong muốn (create desired effects). Việc tạo ra hiệu ứng mong muốn này chính là cách kiểm soát/điều hướng phản ứng của người xem. Ý này khớp với nội dung lựa chọn C.
=> Đáp án: C

questions.prompt: The feelings and motivations of characters become clear
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “feelings and motivations of characters”, “become clear” (trở nên rõ ràng).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn B, câu thứ 2 (vùng bôi xanh lá ở hình 2):

“As is the case with stage drama, dialogue serves to tell the story and expresses feelings and motivations of characters as well.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: Bài đọc chỉ rõ đối thoại (dialogue) có tác dụng kể lại câu chuyện và bộc lộ cảm xúc, động cơ của nhân vật. Điều này có nghĩa là khi khán giả lắng nghe các đoạn hội thoại, những cảm xúc và động cơ đó sẽ trở nên rõ ràng.
=> Đáp án: A

questions.prompt: A character seems to be a real person rather than an actor
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword: “real person rather than an actor” (một con người thật sự chứ không phải diễn viên).

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn: Đoạn C, câu đầu tiên (thông tin trích dẫn ở hình 1):

“When voice textures fit the performer’s physiognomy and gestures, a whole and very realistic persona emerges.”

Bước 3: Phân tích: * Cụm “voice textures fit the performer’s physiognomy and gestures” được paraphrase lại thành ý ở đáp án E: ngoại hình (appearance ~ physiognomy), giọng nói (voice ~ voice textures) và chuyển động (moves ~ gestures) của diễn viên nhất quán, phù hợp với nhau (consistent with each other ~ fit).

Khi có sự nhất quán này, một hình tượng vô cùng chân thực (very realistic persona = a real person rather than an actor) sẽ xuất hiện, khiến người xem cảm thấy họ là một con người bằng xương bằng thịt đang sống cuộc đời của nhân vật chứ không phải một diễn viên đang diễn.
=> Đáp án: E

features.name: when the audience listens to the dialogue
if the film reflects the audience’s own concerns
if voice, sound and music are combined appropriately
when the director is aware of how the audience will respond
when the actor s appearance, voice and moves are consistent with each other

parts.rangeLabel: Read the text and answer questions 27–40.

passage.text:
This Marvellous Invention
A. Of all mankind’s manifold creations, language must take pride of place. Other inventions -the wheel, agriculture, sliced bread – may have transformed our material existence, but the advent of language is what made us human. Compared to language, all other inventions pale in significance, since everything we have ever achieved depends on language and originates from it. Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent to unparalleled power over all other animals, and even over nature itself.
B. But language is foremost not just because it came first. In its own right it is a tool of extraordinary sophistication, yet based on an idea of ingenious simplicity: ‘this marvellous invention of composing out of twenty-five or thirty sounds that infinite variety of expressions which, whilst having in themselves no likeness to what is in our mind, allow us to disclose to others its whole secret, and to make known to those who cannot penetrate it all that we imagine, and all the various stirrings of our soul’. This was how, in 1660, the renowned French grammarians of the Port-Royal abbey near Versailles distilled the essence of language, and no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement. Even so, there is just one flaw in all these hymns of praise, for the homage to languages unique accomplishment conceals a simple yet critical incongruity. Language is mankind s greatest invention – except, of course, that it was never invented. This apparent paradox is at the core of our fascination with language, and it holds many of its secrets.
C. Language often seems so skillfully drafted that one can hardly imagine it as anything other than the perfected handiwork of a master craftsman. How else could this instrument make so much out of barely three dozen measly morsels of sound? In themselves, these configurations of mouth p,f,b,v,t,d,k,g,sh,a,e and so on – amount to nothing more than a few haphazard spits and splutters, random noises with no meaning, no ability to express, no power to explain. But run them through the cogs and wheels of the language machine, let it arrange them in some very special orders, and there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do: from sighing the interminable boredom of existence to unravelling the fundamental order of the universe.
D. The most extraordinary thing about language, however, is that one doesn’t have to be a genius to set its wheels in motion. The language machine allows just about everybody from pre-modern foragers in the subtropical savannah, to post-modern philosophers in the suburban sprawl – to tie these meaningless sounds together into an infinite variety of subtle senses, and all apparently without the slightest exertion. Yet it is precisely this deceptive ease which makes language a victim of its own success, since in everyday life its triumphs are usually taken for granted. The wheels of language run so smoothly that one rarely bothers to stop and think about all the resourcefulness and expertise that must have gone into making it tick. Language conceals art.
E. Often, it is only the estrangement of foreign tongues, with their many exotic and outlandish features, that brings home the wonder of languages design. One of the showiest stunts that some languages can pull off is an ability to build up words of breath-breaking length, and thus express in one word what English takes a whole sentence to say. The Turkish word çehirliliçtiremediklerimizdensiniz, to take one example, means nothing less than ‘you are one of those whom we can’t turn into a town-dweller’. (In case you were wondering, this monstrosity really is one word, not merely many different words squashed together – most ol its components cannot even stand up on their own.)
F. And if that sounds like some one-off freak, then consider Sumerian, the language spoken on the banks of the Euphrates some 5,000 years ago by the people who invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history. A Sumerian word like munintuma’a (‘when he had made it suitable for her’) might seem rather trim compared to the Turkish colossus above. What is so impressive about it, however, is not its lengthiness but rather the reverse – the thrifty compactness of its construction. The word is made up of different slots, each corresponding to a particular portion of meaning. This sleek design allows single sounds to convey useful information, and in fact even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific. If you were to ask which bit in the Sumerian word corresponds to the pronoun ‘it’ in the English translation ‘when he had made it suitable for her’, then the answer would have to be nothing. Mind you, a very particular kind of nothing: the nothing that stands in the empty slot in the middle. The technology is so fine-tuned then that even a non-sound, when carefully placed in a particular position, has been invested with a specific function. Who could possibly have come up with such a nifty contraption?

questionGroups.instruction:
Reading Passage has paragraphs A–F. Choose the correct heading for each paragraph from the list of headings below.
Write the correct number, i–vii, in boxes 27–32 on your answer sheet.

questions.prompt: paragraph A
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
language made us human

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn A:
“the advent of language is what made us human”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Đoạn nói ngôn ngữ là phát minh quan trọng nhất.
=> Đáp án: vi

questions.prompt: paragraph B
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
greatest invention
never invented

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn B:
“language is mankind’s greatest invention – except… it was never invented.”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Hai đặc điểm tưởng như mâu thuẫn cùng tồn tại.
=> Đáp án: iv

questions.prompt: a description of the speed of language processing
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
few sounds
huge range of meaning

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn C:
“there is nothing that these meaningless streams of air cannot do”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Một số ít âm thanh được sắp xếp để tạo vô số ý nghĩa.
=> Đáp án: ii

questions.prompt: a mention of the importance of agreement among speakers
questions.explanation: Bước 1: Keyword everybody without exertion  Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn Đoạn D: “just about everybody… without the slightest exertion”  Bước 3: Phân tích Mọi người đều có khả năng sử dụng ngôn ngữ. => Đáp án: vii

questions.prompt: an explanation of why language is not a 'marvellous invention'
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
foreign tongues
different languages

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn E:
“the estrangement of foreign tongues”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Sự khác biệt giữa các ngôn ngữ làm nổi bật tính ấn tượng của chúng.
=> Đáp án: i

questions.prompt: a mention of the implications of linguistic research
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
absence of a sound

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn F:
“even the absence of a sound has been enlisted to express something specific”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Ngay cả sự im lặng cũng có ý nghĩa.
=> Đáp án: v

questionGroups.note:
What happens when people encounter misinformation?

Although people have 31________ to misinformation, there is debate about precisely how and when we label something as true or untrue. The philosophers Descartes and Spinoza had 32________ about how people engage with information. While Descartes believed that people accept or reject information after considering whether it is true or not, Spinoza argued that people accepted all information they encountered (and by default misinformation) and did not verify or reject it until afterwards. Moreover, Spinoza believed that a distinct 33________ is involved in these stages. Recent research has provided 34________ for Spinoza's theory and it would appear that people accept all encountered information as if it were true, even if this is for an extremely 35________, and do not label the information as true or false until later. This is consistent with the fact that the resources for scepticism and the resources for perceiving and encoding are in 36________ in the brain.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the summary using the list of phrases, A–G, below.

questions.prompt: The wheel is one invention that has had a major impact on ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
impact on aspects of life

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn A:
“transformed our material existence”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Bánh xe ảnh hưởng đến đời sống vật chất.
=> Đáp án: material

questions.prompt: but no impact has been as  ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
impact of language

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn A:
“all other inventions pale in significance”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Không gì quan trọng bằng ngôn ngữ.
=> Đáp án: fundamental

questions.prompt: Language is very  ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
small number of sounds

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn B:
“a tool of extraordinary sophistication”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Ngôn ngữ rất phức tạp/tinh vi.
=> Đáp án: complex

questions.prompt: Language appears to be ___________.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
appears to be

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn D:
“without the slightest exertion”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Ngôn ngữ có vẻ dễ sử dụng.
=> Đáp án: easy

questionGroups.note:
What happens when people encounter misinformation?

Although people have 31________ to misinformation, there is debate about precisely how and when we label something as true or untrue. The philosophers Descartes and Spinoza had 32________ about how people engage with information. While Descartes believed that people accept or reject information after considering whether it is true or not, Spinoza argued that people accepted all information they encountered (and by default misinformation) and did not verify or reject it until afterwards. Moreover, Spinoza believed that a distinct 33________ is involved in these stages. Recent research has provided 34________ for Spinoza's theory and it would appear that people accept all encountered information as if it were true, even if this is for an extremely 35________, and do not label the information as true or false until later. This is consistent with the fact that the resources for scepticism and the resources for perceiving and encoding are in 36________ in the brain.

questionGroups.instruction:
Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in the Reading Passage?
In boxes 37–40 on your answer sheet, write
YES  if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO  if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN  if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

questions.prompt: Human beings might have achieved their present position without language.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
without language

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn A:
“Without language, we could never have embarked on our ascent”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Tác giả cho rằng không có ngôn ngữ thì loài người không đạt được vị trí hiện tại.
=> Đáp án: NO

questions.prompt: The Port-Royal grammarians did justice to the nature of language.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Port-Royal grammarians

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn B:
“no one since has celebrated more eloquently the magnitude of its achievement”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Tác giả đánh giá cao cách họ mô tả ngôn ngữ.
=> Đáp án: YES

questions.prompt: A complex idea can be explained more clearly in a sentence than in a single word.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
single word
sentence

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn E:
“express in one word what English takes a whole sentence to say”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Bài đọc không nói câu văn rõ ràng hơn từ đơn.
=> Đáp án: NOT GIVEN

questions.prompt: The Sumerians were responsible for starting the recording of events.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Sumerians
recording of events

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn văn
Đoạn F:
“invented writing and thus enabled the documentation of history”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Người Sumer phát minh chữ viết, giúp ghi chép lịch sử.
=> Đáp án: YES
